title: 〜はずだ・〜はずがない
description: 〜はずだ expresses a logical expectation — "it should be (so)," based on reasoning or known facts. Its counterpart 〜はずがない means "there's no way / it can't possibly be," a confident denial of a possibility.

content.text: 〜はずだ says that, given what you know, something should logically be the case. It attaches to plain-form verbs and い-adjectives directly, な-adjectives with な, and nouns with の. It reflects reasoned expectation, not certainty or obligation.

content.japanese: Verb・い-adj （plain）／ な-adj ＋ な ／ Noun ＋ の　＋ はずだ
彼（かれ）は約束（やくそく）したから、来（く）るはずだ。
content.english: He promised, so he should come.

content.japanese: この時間（じかん）なら、店（みせ）はまだ開（あ）いているはずだ。
content.english: At this hour the shop should still be open.

content.text: 〜はずがない (also 〜はずはない) flips the expectation into a strong denial: reasoning tells you it simply cannot be true.

content.japanese: Verb・Adjective （plain）／ な-adj ＋ な　＋ はずがない
そんなに簡単（かんたん）なはずがない。
content.english: There's no way it's that easy.

content.japanese: 彼女（かのじょ）がそんな嘘（うそ）をつくはずがない。
content.english: There's no way she would tell such a lie.

content.japanese: 彼（かれ）が来（こ）ないはずがない。絶対（ぜったい）来（く）る。
content.english: There's no way he won't come. He'll definitely be here.

content.text: Don't confuse 〜はずだ ("should be," an expectation about facts) with 〜べきだ ("should do," a moral obligation). 来（く）るはずだ = "he's expected to come"; 来（く）るべきだ = "he ought to come." はず predicts; べき prescribes.

examples.japanese: Verb・い-adj （plain）／ な-adj ＋ な ／ Noun ＋ の　＋ はずだ
彼（かれ）は約束（やくそく）したから、来（く）るはずだ。
examples.english: He promised, so he should come.

examples.japanese: この時間（じかん）なら、店（みせ）はまだ開（あ）いているはずだ。
examples.english: At this hour the shop should still be open.

examples.japanese: Verb・Adjective （plain）／ な-adj ＋ な　＋ はずがない
そんなに簡単（かんたん）なはずがない。
examples.english: There's no way it's that easy.

examples.japanese: 彼女（かのじょ）がそんな嘘（うそ）をつくはずがない。
examples.english: There's no way she would tell such a lie.
